div1.entryFree: Constantly, firmly; 1) with firmness of mind: to meet all perils very c. Caes. V, 1, 92. 2) fixedly: and fixed his eyes upon you? most c. Hml. I, 2, 234. 3) certainly, for certain: I do c. believe you, Meas. IV, 1, 21. I c. do think, Troil. IV, 1, 40. 4) faithfully: since patiently and c. thou hast stuck to the bare fortune of Posthumus, Cymb. III, 5, 119. 5) consistently: the devil a puritan that he is, or any thing c., but a time-pleasor, Tw. II, 3, 160.
